setting.note: The Croatia of the war for independence and of the peace that follows it. The map opens in Zagreb in 1991, a ten-year-old's city of air-raid sirens, shelters and a family flat, then runs south to the killing grounds near the Bosnian border and, a decade on, back to the Dalmatian coast where the narrator returns to face what happened.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Sara Nović, first published 2015. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Girl at War, Sara Nović (2015). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The deep red of the Croatian checkerboard and of the war that runs through the book. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
